findings.description: Page 2 (Our Role) has a two-column layout in the PDF with DEQ/SCDNR screenshots side by side and partner logos arranged in a multi-column grid. The HTML renders these as a single column, stacking elements vertically.
findings.evidence: PDF shows DEQ screenshot and SCDNR screenshot side-by-side in the upper portion, and partner logos in a multi-column grid below. HTML shows all content stacked vertically in a single column.

findings.description: Several partner logos on page 2 appear cropped or partially visible in the HTML version compared to the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows complete logos for Lake Norman Marine Commission, Charlotte-Mecklenburg Storm Water, etc. in full. HTML shows some logos partially cut off (e.g., Lake Norman Marine Commission logo is cropped).

findings.description: Page 4 (Aquatic Plant Report) has a two-column layout in PDF with the report form on the left and key takeaways/agency logos on the right. HTML renders as single column.
findings.evidence: PDF shows form screenshot on left, key takeaways bullet list and DEQ/WRC logos on the right side. HTML stacks these vertically.

findings.description: Page 5 (How to Take a Good ID Photo) has a two-column layout with good photos (green border) on left and bad photos (red border) on right. HTML renders in single column.
findings.evidence: PDF shows green-bordered good examples on left, red-bordered bad examples on right in a clear side-by-side comparison. HTML stacks them vertically, losing the visual comparison effect.

findings.description: Page 6 (How to Get in Touch) has a two-column layout with contact info on the left and 'Stop Aquatic Hitchhikers' poster on the right. HTML renders as single column.
findings.evidence: PDF shows contact emails on left side and Stop Aquatic Hitchhikers infographic on the right side. HTML stacks them vertically.

findings.description: The 'Key takeaways' text is rendered as an h2 heading in the HTML, but in the PDF it appears as a content label/subheading on slide 4, not a major section heading. This is a minor structural choice.
findings.evidence:
PDF slide 4: 'Key takeaways:' as bullet intro text. HTML: '

Key takeaways:

'

findings.description: There are duplicate <main> tags nested inside each other, which is a structural duplication error in the HTML.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '<body><main><main>' - two nested main elements.

findings.description: The PDF page 2 contains visible text from the NC DEQ Aquatic Weed Control Program website screenshot and the SC DNR Aquatic Nuisance Species Program website screenshot with specific program descriptions, purpose statements, objectives, and South Carolina state law citations. These detailed texts are not reproduced in the HTML - they are only represented via image alt text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 includes text like 'The purpose of the Aquatic Weed Control Program is to provide units of local government and residents of North Carolina with resources...' and SC law references 'Title 46, Chapter 23 - South Carolina Noxious Weed Act'. None of this text appears in the HTML body.

findings.description: The descriptive text from the PDF's page 3 website screenshot about aquatic plants in reservoirs and Duke Energy's management program is not included in the HTML as text content, only referenced through the image alt text.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 includes text like 'A key aspect of being a good environmental steward is monitoring and managing our rivers and lakes for aquatic plants and wildlife...' and 'Many invasive aquatic plants have the potential to invade Duke Energy's lakes and rivers...'

findings.description: Several image alt texts on page 2 are reasonable but somewhat generic or approximate, though not inaccurate. The alt text for logos and website screenshots is generally good.
findings.evidence: E.g., 'Belmont North Carolina Logo' and 'Gastonia Logo' - these appear to be logos from the PDF but their exact identity is hard to confirm from context.
